incident: A chart page passed build and API checks but still had visually clipped labels in the screenshot report.
evidence: user statement: Designer owns visual QA
expected_future_effect: Route visible UI acceptance to the UI owner even when backend tests pass.

incident: A UI table looked fine, but displayed stale API data because server-side cache invalidation was wrong.
expected_future_effect: Split visible UI verification from backend data correctness.

incident: A user says 'remember that this provider is broken' without a source, error, date, or affected scope.
expected_future_effect: Return BLOCKED until provenance and scope exist.

incident: A terminal transcript includes a private token while debugging a deployment.
expected_future_effect: Never store raw secrets in durable memory.

incident: An agent repeatedly used a live browser tool where the project visual QA harness would have produced reusable artifacts.
expected_future_effect: Prefer repeatable harness evidence before targeted live-control fallback.

incident: A generated source map suggests two notes are related, but no canonical note confirms the policy.
expected_future_effect: Treat generated indexes as routing hints, not canonical memory truth.

incident: A pricing decision was saved last month, but provider pricing changed this week.
expected_future_effect: Use lifecycle revalidation before relying on pricing memory.

incident: The user states that expensive models should be used only for Oracle, God Coder, or narrow premium design advice.
expected_future_effect: Default frequent memory curation and research to cheaper models unless a risk trigger exists.

incident: The task finished normally with no new root cause, preference, policy, or reusable workflow lesson.
expected_future_effect: Do not save diary-style routine summaries.

incident: A new note says backend agents can approve UI appearance, conflicting with active UI ownership memory.
expected_future_effect: Preserve both positions and ask the lead agent to resolve meaning.

incident: A user preference affects many future tasks and should be loaded cheaply at session start.
expected_future_effect: Add to Hot Context Pack only with source paths and lifecycle metadata.

incident: A large vault contains many unrelated notes; only two prior decisions should affect the new task.
expected_future_effect: Return a bounded Brain Brief instead of broad vault dumps.
